description: In an apartment kitchen, Chandler pushes a foosball table across the floor.
Joey, wearing a dark coat and a striped scarf, enters through the front door.
Joey says, "Hey, hold on a second." Chandler stops pushing the table and stands up straight.
Joey closes the door, removes his scarf, and pulls a white envelope from his coat pocket. Chandler walks toward him.
Chandler smiles and says, "Nice."
Joey holds the envelope out toward Chandler.

Joey hands the envelope over, saying, "Hey, I got something for you." Chandler opens it and asks, "What's this?" Joey replies, "$812." Chandler looks down at the contents, then up, saying, "Well, I don't know what Big Leon told you, but it's an even"
Chandler finishes his line, "thousand if you want me for the whole night." Joey smiles and shifts his weight.
Joey raises his hands slightly while smiling.
Chandler asks, "What is this for?" Joey gestures with his hands and explains, "Well, making money now. And this is paying you back for headshots, electric bills and so many"
Joey continues, "slices of pizza I can't even count."
Joey says, "I love you, man." Chandler looks down at the envelope and replies, "Well, thanks, man."
Chandler turns slightly away, smiling.

Chandler walks toward the living area, saying, "Now I can get my pony." Joey pulls a small black box from his pocket and holds it out. "Hey, this is a little extra something for always being there for me."
Chandler turns back and takes the box from Joey.
Chandler opens the box and says, "Wow, I don't know what to say."
Chandler pulls a thick gold bracelet from the box, letting it dangle from his fingers. He stares at it.
Looking at the bracelet, Chandler repeats, "It wow, I don't know what to say."
Joey smiles broadly, looking at Chandler.
Joey asks, "What do you say?"
Chandler replies, "I don't know."
Chandler keeps the bracelet suspended in the air, his gaze fixed on it.
Joey says, "It's a bracelet, isn't it?" He steps closer and points at the back of it. "And it's a grave too. Check it out."
Joey reads from the bracelet, "To my best bud."
Chandler lowers the bracelet back into the box and closes the lid.
Chandler says, "Thanks, best bud," and briefly touches Joey's arm.
Chandler turns and walks toward a wooden chair.
Joey gestures toward Chandler, saying, "Put it on." Chandler stops, looks back, and says, "Oh, now?"
Chandler sets the wooden chair down on the floor.
Chandler waves his hands. "No. I think something this nice should be safe for a special occasion." Joey steps toward him. "Oh, no, that's the beauty part. It goes with everything."
Joey points at Chandler and says, "You put this on, you're good to go."
Chandler lowers his head slightly, then looks back at Joey.
Chandler says, "Well, I've never been good to go, so what the hell, why not try some jewelry?"
Joey takes the bracelet and fastens it around Chandler's right wrist.
Chandler looks at his wrist. Joey says, "Oh, man, you are so wearing that bracelet."
Chandler holds up his wrist, looking at the bracelet, and says, "I so am."
Joey points toward Chandler's wrist.
Joey asks, "You have any idea what this will do for your sex life?" Chandler looks at the bracelet. "Well, it'll probably slow it down at first, but once I get used to the extra weight,"
Chandler gestures with his right hand. "I'll be back on track."
The screen goes dark.
The setting changes to a restaurant kitchen. A woman with dark hair walks alongside the Restaurant Manager, who holds a piece of paper. On-screen text reads: "SUPERVISING PRODUCER MICHAEL BORKOW".
The manager says, "Well, this all looks good." The woman replies, "Great."
The manager asks, "And if I want to call for reference on your last job?" The woman points at the paper. "Oh, that's there on the bottom. See?" On-screen text reads: "PRODUCED BY TODD STEVENS".

The manager reads, "The manager, Chandler Bing." He lowers the paper and says, "All right, let's see if you're as good in person as you are on paper. Make me a" On-screen text reads: "PRODUCER BETSY BORNS".

The manager finishes, "salad." The woman asks, "A salad? Really? I could do something a little more complicated, if you like." On-screen text reads: "WRITTEN BY ALEXA JUNGE". The manager responds, "No, just a salad will be fine." On-screen text reads: "DIRECTED BY JAMES BURROWS".
The woman claps her hands together, smiles, and steps toward a white prep table covered with vegetables.
The woman says, "You got it," and reaches for a head of lettuce.
The manager steps back and sits on a stool. "Now, I want you to tell me what you're doing while you're doing it." The woman picks up the lettuce. "All right. Well,"

The woman pulls leaves off the lettuce. "I'm tearing the lettuce." The manager leans forward slightly. "Is it dirty?" The woman glances at him. "Oh, no. Don't worry. I'm going to wash it." The manager says, "Don't. I like it dirty."
The woman pauses her hand movements and looks at the manager.
In a restaurant kitchen, Monica stands at a prep table tearing lettuce leaves. The Restaurant Manager sits on a stool behind her.
The manager says, "That's your call."
The manager asks, "So, what are you going to do next?" Monica places the lettuce in a metal bowl.
Monica wipes her hands, reaches toward a tray of tomatoes, and says, "Well, I thought that I would cut up the tomatoes."
The manager asks, "Are they firm?" his voice low. Audience laughter starts.
Monica picks up a tomato, turns to look at the manager, and smiles briefly. She presses her thumb into the tomato.
Monica says, "They're all right." The manager leans forward slightly and asks, "You sure they haven't gone bad?" Audience laughter continues.
The manager continues, "You sure they're not very, very bad?" his pace slower.
Monica stops smiling, puts the tomato down, and turns to face the manager, her expression neutral.
Monica says, "No, really, they're okay." The manager asks, "You gonna slice them up real nice?"
Monica turns back to the prep table, holding her knife.
Monica glances back at him and says, "Actually, I was gonna do them julienne."
The manager tips his head back, closes his eyes, and exhales audibly. Audience laughter is heard.
Monica drops the knife onto the cutting board, steps back quickly, and says, "I'm out of here."
Monica walks quickly away from the prep table, exiting the frame. The manager opens his eyes and looks in her direction.
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Chandler sits on a couch looking at a sparkly bracelet on his wrist, while Phoebe sits nearby holding a bowl.
The view shifts to the kitchen area. Ross sits at a round table, flipping through mail, as a cordless phone rings. He picks up the phone.
Ross puts the phone to his ear and says, "Yellow?"
Ross listens, holding a pen over a notepad.
Ross says, "No, Rachel's not here right now. Can I take a message?"
Ross says, "All right," and begins writing on the notepad.
Ross asks, "And how do we spell Casey? Is it like at the Bat or and the Sunshine Band?" Chandler turns his head to look over the back of the couch toward Ross.
Ross nods slightly and stops writing.
Ross says, "Okay. Bye bye," and presses a button on the phone.
Ross sets the phone down, tears the piece of paper from the notepad, and stands up.
Ross walks toward the refrigerator holding the note. Chandler asks, "Hey, who's this Casey?" Ross replies, "Oh, some guy she met at the movies."
Chandler asks, "Oh, really? What does he want with her?" Ross says, "Well, I'm guessing"
Ross pins the note to the refrigerator with a magnet and continues, "he wants to do a little"
Ross turns around, hums a rhythmic tune, and does a brief shimmy with his shoulders and arms. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross points at Chandler and says, "know, make a little love."
Ross lowers his hand and walks past the couch. Phoebe chuckles. Chandler watches Ross, his expression neutral.
Ross stops, moves his arms again, and says, "I'll pretty much get down tonight."
Ross finishes his dance and walks back toward the refrigerator as audience laughter continues.
Ross reaches up and opens a cabinet door above the refrigerator. He looks inside, then closes it.
Ross turns around and says, "I don't know."
Ross stands by the refrigerator, gesturing with his hands. "I don't get it. I mean, like, two months ago, Rachel and I were, like, this close."
Ross lowers his hands and says, "Right now what? I'm taking messages from"
Ross walks from the kitchen area toward the living room, passing behind the couch. "guys she meets at the movies."
He continues walking, saying, "I mean this casey should be taking down my messages or Rachel and I should"
Ross stops near the hallway entrance, waving his hands. "be together and we should get some kind of message service."
Ross lowers his hands. Phoebe, seated in the armchair, turns her head to look at him.
Phoebe tilts her head and says, "Hang in there. It's going to happen."
Ross asks, "Okay, now, how do you know that?" Phoebe replies, "Because she's your lobster."
Ross stares at Phoebe with furrowed brows. Chandler turns to look at Phoebe. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler says, "Oh, she's going somewhere."
Phoebe leans forward in her chair and gestures with both hands.
Phoebe says, "Come on, you guys. It's a known fact that lobsters fall in love and mate for life."
Phoebe continues, "And you know what? You can actually see old"
Phoebe mimics lobster claws with her hands, moving them in front of her. "lobster couples walking around their tanks, holding claws."
Phoebe continues to move her hands like claws. Ross looks at her with a blank expression, and Chandler stares downward. Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe says, "Yes, you have to picture lobsters." Audience laughter continues.
Chandler maintains a neutral expression, gazing downward.
Monica, wearing a red bathrobe with a white towel wrapped around her head, enters from the hallway. Chandler stands up from the couch.
Chandler asks, "Hey, you feeling better?" Monica walks past him toward the kitchen. "Yeah, I think that fifth shower actually got the interview off me."
Monica stops at the refrigerator and pulls the door open.
Chandler asks, "So do you have any other possibilities?" Monica takes a bottle from the fridge and closes the door.
Monica walks over to Ross at the table and says, "Oh, yeah. Well, there is the possibility that I won't make rent."
Ross turns to Monica and says, "If you want, I can lend you some money."
Monica twists the cap onto the bottle and says, "No, because if I couldn't pay you back right away then I'd feel guilty and tense every time I saw you."
Ross replies, "Okay, well,"
Ross points his finger and says, "then why don't you borrow it from mom and dad? You feel guilty and tense around them already." Audience laughter is heard.
Monica stops moving the bottle and looks directly at Ross.
Ross spreads his hands and says, "You might as well make some money off of them."
Monica presses her lips together in a brief smile. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler gestures toward Ross and says, "You know, the man's got a point."
Monica turns her head toward Chandler, who raises his right hand slightly, revealing a gold bracelet on his wrist.
Monica steps toward Chandler and asks, "What is that sparkly thing?" pointing at his wrist.
Chandler lowers his hand, glancing at his wrist.
Chandler says, "That thing?"
Chandler says, "It's a yacht."
Chandler lowers his arms.

In an apartment kitchen, Chandler holds his right wrist up, displaying a thick gold bracelet. Ross stands next to him looking at it, and Monica stands behind them wearing a red bathrobe with a towel wrapped around her head.
Ross says, "It's a little flashy," looking at the bracelet.
Chandler looks down at the bracelet.
Ross shakes his head and says, "No,"
Ross continues looking at Chandler's wrist.
Ross taps the bracelet with his finger and says, "it's not flashy. Not for a good fella."
Chandler lifts his wrist higher, inspecting the jewelry.
Ross looks from the bracelet to Chandler and says, "Man, that is sharp. That must have cost you quite a few dubloons." Audience laughter is audible.
The setting shifts to the adjacent living room area as Phoebe enters.
Phoebe looks at the bracelet and says, "No, it's fun."
Phoebe gestures with both hands as she adds, "It's fun. It's kind of like knowing one of the Gabor sisters," over audience laughter.
Chandler looks at Phoebe while Monica and Ross stand behind him smiling.
An exterior daytime view shows a city street with traffic and the World Trade Center towers in the distance.
Now inside the apartment living room, Monica, wearing an orange shirt and jeans, opens the front door. Jack enters carrying a cardboard box, followed by Judy who also carries boxes.
Monica asks, "So, what's this?" Jack replies, "Some of your old stuff."
Judy sets a box down on the coffee table and says, "Well, sweetie, we have a surprise for you."
Judy claps her hands together and says, "We're turning your room into a jam."
Monica looks at Judy with her eyes widened.
Monica gestures with her hands and asks, "Wow, that is a surprise. Just one little question. Why not Ross's room?"
Jack replies, "Gosh, we talked about that. But your brother has so many"
Jack gestures as he continues, "science trophies and plaques and merit badges. Well, we didn't want to disturb them," over audience laughter.
Monica spreads her arms out and says, "God forbid." Audience laughter plays again.
Judy stands with her hands clasped, smiling.
Judy asks Monica, "What's new with you? What can you tell us? How's your job?"
Monica asks, "My job?"
Monica says, "Well"
Monica rubs her hands together.
Monica points at herself and says, "did I mention I still don't have a boyfriend?"
Jack looks downward.
Jack says, "Oh, well, I just assume," as the audience laughs.
The front door opens and Rachel walks in carrying a white laundry basket.
Judy opens her arms and says, "Oh, hi, Rachel."
Judy steps forward and places her hands on Rachel's arms.
Judy says, "We were so sorry to hear about your parents splitting up, dear."
Rachel looks down and replies, "Oh, well, you know, they're just, you know, never know. We'll see. Well,"
Jack crosses his arms and says, "I can't say any of us were surprised."
Rachel and Judy both turn their heads to look at Jack.
Jack says, "Your parents have been unhappy ever since we've known them."
Jack adds, "Especially after that incident in Hawaii," followed by audience laughter.
Rachel sets the laundry basket down.
Rachel asks, "What incident?"
Jack touches his temple and says, "No, I must be thinking of someone else."
Jack points to his chest and says, "Maybe me," over audience laughter.
Jack gestures toward the laundry basket.
Jack steps toward Rachel and says, "Don't you have some folding to do? Go fold, dear. Fold."
Jack gently pushes Rachel toward the hallway, saying, "You fold."
Outside the Central Perk coffee shop, a woman walks past the window. Inside, Chandler sits on a couch.
Phoebe approaches Chandler holding a coffee pot and asks, "Do you want to refill?"
Chandler continues looking at a menu and says, "No, I'm all right."
A woman in a red shirt, Gail, sits on a sofa nearby and looks toward Chandler. Phoebe turns toward Chandler.
Phoebe says, "Okay."
Phoebe says, "You got to give me a second. I want to get this just right," as she adjusts her sweater.
Phoebe shakes her head, tosses her hair, and steps back toward Chandler.
Phoebe leans down over Chandler's shoulder and softly says, "Dude, 11:00 totally hot babe checking you out."
Chandler looks over his shoulder toward Gail.
Chandler looks back at Phoebe and says, "That was really good."
Phoebe smiles and says, "Think I'm ready for my peanut snap."
Chandler glances over his shoulder again. Gail meets his gaze and smiles.
Chandler stands up, adjusts his vest, walks over to Gail, and leans his arm on the back of a chair near her table.
Chandler points at his own head and says, "I know what you're thinking."
Chandler says, "Dave Thomas, founder of Wendy's." Gail and the audience laugh.
Gail continues laughing and leans forward.
Gail says, "I'm Gail." Chandler replies, "I'm Chandler."
Chandler raises his hand toward his face, exposing his gold bracelet. Gail stops smiling, stands up quickly, and picks up her jacket.
Gail says, "I really have to be somewhere, but it was nice meeting you," and walks past him as audience laughter plays.
Chandler watches her walk away and says, "What"
Chandler lowers his gaze to his wrist.
Chandler stares at the bracelet and points at it with his other hand.
Chandler turns and walks back toward Phoebe, saying, "is excellent."
Chandler moves his hands quickly and says, "You know, he could have got me a VCR. He could have got me a set of golf clubs. But no, he has to get me the woman repeller," as audience laughter builds.
Chandler taps the bracelet with force.
Chandler raises his volume and says, "The eyesore from the Liberace House of Crap."
Phoebe holds a coffee mug and looks at the bracelet.
Phoebe says, "Not that bad." Chandler points at his wrist and says, "Oh, yeah. Easy for you to say. You don't have to walk around sporting some reject from the Mr."
Joey enters through the front door and approaches behind Chandler as Chandler finishes, "T collection," while the audience laughs.
Chandler notices Joey standing directly behind him.
Chandler points his finger and says, "I pity the fool who puts on my jewelry. I do," speaking in a lower pitch over audience laughter.
Chandler nods at Joey.
Chandler walks past Joey, repeating, "I pity the fool"
Chandler walks out of frame as Joey watches him.
In the Central Perk coffee shop, Chandler stands near the counter while Phoebe sits on the arm of the sofa, holding a blue mug. Chandler turns toward the door.
Chandler gestures toward the door and says, "hey, man, we were just doing some impressions over here."
Chandler places a hand on his chest and says, "Dear Marcel marceau."
Chandler raises his hands flat in the air, mimicking pressing against an invisible wall. A man in a dark jacket walks past him in the background.
Lowering his hands, Chandler says, "That's actually good."

The setting changes to a view of the New York City skyline with the Empire State Building visible under a clear sky. The scene returns to Central Perk, where Rachel stands at the counter talking to a man in a dark green shirt.
Ross sits on the sofa beside Phoebe, who holds an acoustic guitar. Ross gestures toward Rachel and the man, his gaze fixed on them.
Ross asks, "Would you look at that guy? I mean, how long has he been talking to her?"
Phoebe answers, "I don't know. A couple of minutes." Ross replies, "Yeah, try doubling that."
Ross gestures with his hand toward the counter.
Ross says, "It's like, back off, budy."
Still looking toward the counter, Ross adds, "She's a waitress, not a geisha."
Phoebe shifts her position slightly on the sofa.
Phoebe says, "I think she's okay."
At the counter, Rachel touches the man's arm and lowers her head slightly.
Ross points his hand forward and says, "Look at that. Look at that. See how she's pushing him away and he won't budge?"
Ross pats his knees, saying, "All right, I'm going to do something," and stands up from the sofa.
Ross walks toward the counter.
The man, facing Rachel, says, "Get a condo right on the mountainside. Ski in, ski out. It's great."
Ross steps up beside the man, interrupting their conversation.
Ross asks, "Excuse me? Are you Rachel?"
Rachel turns her head toward Ross and asks, "What?"
Ross places a hand on his chest.
Ross says, "Ross Geller."
Rachel and the man look at Ross.
Ross looks Rachel up and down and says, "God, in your ad, you said you were pretty, but wow."
Rachel furrows her eyebrows, staring at Ross.
Rachel asks in a softer tone, "What are you doing?"
Ross points a thumb toward himself, looking at the man, and asks, "Oh, my God. Is this the wrong day?"
Ross shakes his head slightly.
Ross says, "I don't believe it. Well, hey, I guess if it works out, we'll have somebody to tell the grandkids."
The man smiles and replies, "You sure will."
The man steps back and says, "I've got to go. Take care."
Rachel smiles slightly and replies, "Okay. See you later."
The man says, "Nice meeting you," as he turns to leave.
The man walks toward the exit while Ross watches him, a slight smile on his face.
Ross turns back to Rachel and says, "You're welcome."
Rachel crosses her arms and stares at Ross.
Rachel asks, "What?" Ross points to himself and says, "I was saving you."
Rachel gestures with her hand and replies, "Save me? Save me from the pleasant conversation with the interesting man."
Ross gestures vaguely and says, "Oh, see, from where I was sitting"
Rachel points her finger at him and says, "okay, Ross, listen to me. I am not yours to save." Ross replies, "But you"
Ross finishes, "are." Rachel asks, "What?"
Ross steps slightly closer and says, "Well, you're my lobster."
Rachel stares at him, her mouth slightly open. Phoebe sits on the sofa in the background, plucking her guitar.
Rachel asks, "Okay, you know what? Are you being, like the blind date guy again?"
Ross shakes his head.
Ross says, "No, you're my lobster. See, lobsters"
Ross raises his hands in front of his chest.
Ross moves his hands in small circles and says, "in the tank, when they're old,"
Ross continues gesturing with his hands.
Ross forms his fingers into pincer shapes and says, "they walk around holding the claws."
Ross steps closer, linking his pincer-shaped hands together to demonstrate.
Ross says, "In the tank"
Ross turns his head toward Phoebe.
Looking toward Phoebe, Ross says, "with the holding and"
Ross gestures toward her and continues, "phoebes, you want to help me out with the whole lobster thing?"
Phoebe raises her hands, forming pincers with her fingers, and says, "Do the claws again."
Rachel turns and walks away to the other side of the counter. Ross follows her.
Ross leans against the counter and says, "Rach. Okay, forget the lobsters, okay? Let's talk."
Rachel asks, "What about us? Ross, there is no us, okay?"
Rachel gestures with her hands and says, "No, but no, listen to me. I fell"
Rachel places her hand on her chest and says, "for you and I get clobbered. You then fall for me and I again somehow get clobbered."
Rachel adds, "I'm tired of being"
Rachel continues, "clobbered, you know?"
Rachel throws her hands out and says, "It's just not worth it." Ross says, "Well, no, but," and Rachel interrupts, "Ross."
Rachel shakes her head and says, "We are never going to happen."
Ross stares at her in silence.
Ross says in a lower volume, "Okay, except that."
Rachel maintains her gaze on him.
Rachel replies firmly, "Except that."
Ross looks down briefly, then back up at Rachel.
Ross shakes his head slightly and says, "No,"
Ross continues to look at her.
Ross says, "accept that."
Ross looks downward slightly as the segment ends.
The image of Ross quickly darkens until the screen is completely black. Upbeat acoustic guitar transition music plays.
The screen remains completely black. The acoustic guitar music decreases in volume and ends, leaving silence.
